# Strait -- Full Reference

> Open-source job orchestration for background jobs and workflows. Strait runs as a single Go binary backed by PostgreSQL and Redis. Customer job code runs on customer infrastructure.

For a short index, see https://app.strait.dev/llms.txt.

## What Strait Does

Strait lets teams define jobs, trigger runs, retry failures, schedule recurring work, coordinate workflow steps, and inspect execution history. Jobs can run through HTTP endpoints or private workers connected to Strait.

Strait is useful when you need more than a message queue or cron:

- clear run history
- retries and `dead_letter` recovery
- schedules with time zones and overlap controls
- workflow steps with approvals and waits
- live updates, webhooks, audit events, and logs
- SDKs for TypeScript, Python, Go, Ruby, and Rust

## Getting Started

- Introduction: https://docs.strait.dev/introduction
- Choose Your Path: https://docs.strait.dev/choose-your-path
- Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/quickstart
- Create a Production Job: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/production-job

## Core Concepts

### Jobs

Jobs define work Strait can trigger. A job includes the endpoint or worker target, retry policy, timeout, schedule, concurrency settings, and metadata.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/jobs

### Runs

Runs are individual executions of a job. A run usually moves from `queued` to `executing` to `completed`. Failed runs retry according to the job policy. If retries are exhausted, the run moves to `dead_letter` for inspection and replay.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/runs
Related: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/handle-failed-runs

### Workflows

Workflows coordinate multiple jobs and workflow steps. Steps can run in order or in parallel, wait for approvals, pause for external events, and recover failed branches with compensation steps.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/workflows
Related: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/workflow-approvals

### Execution Modes

HTTP jobs call an endpoint you expose. Worker jobs keep your code inside a private network by using a long-lived worker connection to receive work.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/execution-modes
Related: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/private-workers

## SDKs

Use SDKs to trigger jobs, report progress, manage runs, and define workflows from application code.

- Overview: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/overview
- Configuration: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/configuration
- TypeScript Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/typescript-quickstart
- Python Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/python-quickstart
- Go Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/go-quickstart

- Python: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/python
- Go: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/go
- Ruby: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/ruby
- Rust: https://docs.strait.dev/sdks/rust
